markets.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $80,000 on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Paris be 14°C or higher on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors a lowest temperature of 11°C (38.5% implied probability) for Paris on May 1, driven by the latest Météo-France and ECMWF ensemble forecasts indicating overnight lows around 10–12°C amid persistent mild spring conditions in Île-de-France. Recent developments include a week of stable high-pressure influence since late April, with observed minimums of 8–12°C and no cold fronts disrupting the pattern, allowing radiative cooling under partly cloudy skies and light winds. GFS models show similar consensus, though slight cloudiness could push lows toward 10°C (24.5%). Historical May 1 averages hover near 9–10°C, but current warmer anomalies position 11–12°C as leaders; final pre-dawn observations from official stations will resolve amid typical forecast uncertainty.

markets.question: Tari Eason: Points O/U 2.5
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Lakers hold a commanding 3-1 series lead over the Rockets in the Western Conference first-round playoffs, hosting critical Game 5 at Crypto.com Arena amid key injury developments shaping trader consensus. Houston's Kevin Durant is officially ruled out with a left ankle sprain—his latest setback after a prior knee contusion sidelined him early—severely hampering their offense against elimination. Los Angeles remains without Luka Doncic (left hamstring strain), but Austin Reaves is questionable with oblique strain and sources indicate optimism for his return, potentially adding scoring punch alongside LeBron James. Lakers' resilience despite shorthanded lineups, combined with home-court advantage and Rockets' back-to-back struggles, underscores the implied probabilities favoring a closeout.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Stars FC hosts NJ/NY Gotham FC in NWSL regular-season play on April 30, with both squads hampered by key absences per the March 28 availability report. Chicago misses forward Jordyn Huitema (hip), season-ending knee injury to defender Natalia Kuikka, and Mallory Swanson (maternity leave), contributing to their leaky defense conceding 2.5 goals per game amid a recent 0-2 loss to San Diego Wave FC. Gotham lacks attacker Jaedyn Shaw (hamstring), midfielder Bruninha (hamstring), and defender Mandy Freeman (lower leg), reflected in their low-scoring form (1-2-1 record, ninth in standings) including a scoreless draw at Orlando Pride. Home advantage at SeatGeek Stadium and mutual offensive struggles could yield a tight, low-goal affair as traders weigh roster health and early-season momentum.

markets.question: Game 2: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 2. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Kaufland Hangry Knights vs ROSSMANN Centaurs (BO3) - Prime League 1st Division Group A
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Kaufland Hangry Knights and ROSSMANN Centaurs in the Prime League 1st Division Group A, initially scheduled for April 29 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Kaufland Hangry Knights" if Kaufland Hangry Knights win the match against ROSSMANN Centaurs.

This market will resolve to "ROSSMANN Centaurs" if ROSSMANN Centaurs win the match against Kaufland Hangry Knights.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Prime League 1st Division Spring 2026 Group A group stage, Kaufland Hangry Knights enter with a 1-1 series record and +1 map differential after a 1-2 loss to Unicorns of Love SE, buoyed by jungler Densi's recent addition on April 8 and a 1-0 head-to-head win over ROSSMANN Centaurs on April 9. ROSSMANN Centaurs sit winless at 0-2 series, swept 0-4 in maps including a 0-2 defeat to Berlin International Gaming last week, reflecting ongoing struggles in the current patch meta with poor early-game execution. KHK's roster stability featuring top laner Venour, mid Abbedagge, and ADC Exofeng provides matchup edges in BO3 format, though ROSS could exploit any macro missteps amid elimination pressure.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the WTA 125 Huzhou Open round of 16 on outdoor clay, No. 249 seed Polona Hercog meets No. 368 Yufei Ren in their first head-to-head clash. Hercog grinded out a resilient 4-6, 7-6(5), 6-3 upset over No. 8 Xinyu Gao in the opener yesterday, extending her YTD record to 15-7 amid steady challenger form. Ren, the 20-year-old Chinese local, cruised 6-2, 6-1 past Yidi Yang for her 25th win this year (24-7, mostly ITF/WTT level). Hercog's experience and ranking edge shape trader consensus on hard courts transitioning to clay, but Ren's youth, momentum, and home crowd support an upset amid potential weather variability.

Ongoing Palmeiras injuries—Piquerez (ankle surgery), Vitor Roque (ankle), Jefté (thigh)—temper dominance without derailing favoritism, while limited head-to-head history leaves room for Remo's 21.5% upset potential in Belém's humid conditions.

markets.question: Israel x Hezbollah Ceasefire extended by April 26, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the 10-day ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah announced on April 16, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between Israel and Hezbollah, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 16 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect.

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the Israeli government and Hezbollah that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed 10-day period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli government and Hezbollah. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah hostilities that reignited on March 2, 2026, took effect April 17 for 10 days to facilitate negotiations but saw mutual violations including Israeli airstrikes on rocket launchers in southern Lebanon and Hezbollah drone incursions. President Trump announced a three-week extension on April 24 following White House ambassador-level talks, with Israel insisting on a reinforced security zone north of the border and rejecting full withdrawal or "quiet for quiet" terms. Hezbollah deems the truce meaningless amid ongoing strikes, underscoring trader uncertainty over sustained de-escalation ahead of the May mid-extension deadline and potential UNIFIL involvement.

markets.question: Will JD Vance say "King" or "Queen" on the Will Cain Show?
markets.description:
JD Vance is scheduled to appear on the Will Cain Show on April 29, 2026. You can read more about that here: https://x.com/WillCainShow/status/2049245211012870401

This market will resolve to "Yes" if JD Vance says the listed term during this appearance. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old interviews or prerecorded videos are aired where Vance is speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this interview is definitively cancelled by JD Vance or the Will Cain Show, or otherwise is not released by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If the interview has not aired in its entirety by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on what has aired up to that point.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution. Transcripts will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amiens SC, mired in 18th place in Ligue 2 with a dismal home record featuring just two wins from 15 matches, hosts fifth-placed Red Star FC on May 2 in a crucial relegation scrap. Amiens' recent form underscores their woes—a 0-1 home defeat to Pau FC on April 10, a 1-1 draw at Bastia three days prior, and ongoing absences of attacking midfielder Victor Lobry (cruciate ligament tear since November) and left-back Arvin Appiah (Achilles injury). Red Star enters with momentum from a 4-3 win over Bastia, solidifying their playoff push despite Amiens' 3-1 victory in the reverse fixture last August. Head-to-head favors Amiens (7 wins in 13), but Red Star's superior goal difference and depth tilt trader consensus amid late-season stakes.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will Gold (XAUUSD) hit (HIGH) $5,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for Gold (XAUUSD) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session as listed on Pyth will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Gold (XAUUSD) does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Gold (XAUUSD) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Metal.XAU%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the relevant CME COMEX futures contract for the underlying metal—COMEX Gold Futures (GC)—may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Gold (XAUUSD) hit in April 2026?
events.description: What will Gold (XAUUSD) hit in April 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold spot (XAUUSD) trades around $4,558 per ounce as of April 30, reflecting trader consensus driven by persistent central bank purchases—Poland leading with over 20 tonnes added year-to-date—and a weakening US Dollar Index near 97.5, amplifying its inflation-hedge and safe-haven status amid geopolitical risks. The rebound from March's 10% plunge, the steepest since 2013, stems from softer core CPI readings at 2.6% and April FOMC signals of monetary restraint despite elevated oil pressures. Market-implied odds hinge on May nonfarm payrolls, CPI data, and the next policy meeting, with Treasury yields and dollar strength as key swing factors potentially capping upside near $4,700 resistance.

markets.question: X banned in any European country by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the use of X/Twitter is banned within any European country by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a “European country” is defined as any of the following sovereign states: Albania, Andorra, Austria, Belarus, Belgium, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czechia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Georgia, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Ireland, Italy, Kosovo, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Moldova, Monaco, Montenegro, Netherlands, North Macedonia, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, San Marino, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Turkey, Ukraine, United Kingdom, and Vatican City.

A ban will qualify if legislation is enacted or government action is taken to bar the respective country's citizens from downloading and/or viewing X/Twitter, and/or posting on X/Twitter. Any legislation or government action that meets these standards will qualify, regardless of whether or when the ban goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the respective government and X/Twitter; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 75.5% implied probability for X facing a full ban in any European country by year-end, driven by the absence of national-level enforcement actions despite ongoing Digital Services Act (DSA) tensions. The European Commission's €120 million fine in December 2025 targeted X's transparency failures around blue check verification, but X appealed to the EU General Court in February 2026 and submitted a compliance plan by March, averting escalation. Brussels has cautioned member states against unilateral social media crackdowns, prioritizing fines over outright bans amid free speech debates. Key catalysts include the fine appeal outcome and potential DSA probes into content moderation, though historical precedents suggest regulatory pressure stops short of platform-wide prohibitions.

markets.question: Will Brann win Norway Eliteserien?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club is determined as the champion of the Norway Eliteserien. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to be named as the champion of Norway Eliteserien per the rules of Norway Eliteserien (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after December 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the Norway Eliteserien; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Sandefjord (47.4%) and Fredrikstad (47.2%) as virtual co-favorites to win the Eliteserien, with Bodø/Glimt (44.0%) and Kristiansund (43.1%) nipping at their heels in a bunched top tier, highlighting the razor-thin margins defining this title race. Early-season volatility has kept the table compact after 6-8 matchdays, as Sandefjord's recent home draws against Rosenborg and resilient away results, coupled with Fredrikstad's attacking output despite mixed results, have surged their standings into contention around 7 points. Bodø/Glimt rebounded with a 3-0 win over Aalesund last weekend, while Kristiansund's mixed bag including a 3-2 upset at Brann adds unpredictability. Favorable remaining schedules, home form splits, and looming head-to-heads among the pack sustain the tight dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the Hackney mayoral election one week away on May 7, trader consensus heavily favors Green Party candidate Zoë Garbett at 85% implied probability, extrapolating from council election projections where Greens are forecast to secure the most seats (around 25 of 57 per Pollcheck and YouGov MRP models showing Labour-Greens ties at 36%). Incumbent Labour Mayor Caroline Woodley, elected in the 2023 by-election, trails at 15.5% amid voter concerns over housing affordability, cost-of-living pressures, and the controversial temporary closure of Ridley Road market. Garbett's campaign emphasizes progressive alternatives to Labour's 24-year dominance, while Conservatives' Tareke Gregg, Liberal Democrats' Eva Steinhardt, and Reform UK's Vahid Almasi remain marginal. Late turnout or national trends could shift dynamics under first-past-the-post voting.

markets.question: Spread: AJ Auxerre (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 3 at 11:15 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "AJ Auxerre" if AJ Auxerre win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Angers SCO".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: AJ Auxerre vs. Angers SCO - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 3 at 11:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: AJ Auxerre, languishing in 16th in Ligue 1 after 29 matches with just five wins, host mid-table 13th-placed Angers SCO on May 3 in a key relegation six-pointer at Stade de l'Abbé-Deschamps. Auxerre showed grit in recent draws—a 2-2 at Monaco on April 19 and 0-0 versus Nantes on April 11—bolstering survival hopes amid patchy home form. Angers, drawing 1-1 with Le Havre on April 18, grapple with a depleted defense: Yassin Belkhdim (arm fracture, season over), Harouna Djibirin (ligament, out long-term), Carlens Arcus (groin), and Marius Courcoul absent, Haris Belkebla doubtful per April 16 updates. Angers lead head-to-head recently (2-0 win November 2025), but Auxerre's rest edge post-Lyon trip versus Angers' PSG clash could shift trader consensus toward a tight contest.

markets.question: Will Opendoor (OPEN) close at $2.00-$3.00 on the final day of trading of the week of Apr 27 – May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Opendoor (OPEN) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Opendoor (OPEN) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OPEN/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Aryna Sabalenka leads trader consensus at 26.5% implied probability as world No. 1, bolstered by her recent Madrid Open title over Coco Gauff just days ago, demonstrating enhanced clay-court versatility that enhances her powerful serve-and-groundstrokes game suited to grass. Elena Rybakina trails closely at 21% after capturing the Australian Open crown versus Sabalenka in January and maintaining No. 2 ranking with strong hard-court results, leveraging her prior Wimbledon triumph and flat-hitting style ideal for fast grass. Iga Świątek holds 15.3% despite slipping to No. 4 following an early Indian Wells exit, her baseline dominance tempered by historical grass struggles. The tight top cluster reflects deep field uncertainty ahead of grass swing, with recent clay earners like Elise Mertens, Yulia Putintseva, and Amanda Anisimova gaining traction via consistent deep runs and upset potential on variable surfaces.

markets.question: Will Noah Kahan have the second-greatest number of monthly Spotify listeners this month?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed artist with the second greatest number of monthly listeners according to Spotify on April 30, 2026, 12PM ET.

The monthly listener count is listed on each artist's public Spotify profile. Only primary artist profiles will qualify; features or collaborations under another artist profile will not count towards the featured artist's total.

In the event of an exact tie for the number of monthly listeners, this market will resolve in favor of the listed artist whose name comes first in alphabetical order.

If Spotify is down at the listed time on the listed date, this market will resolve based on the most recent available data.

The resolution source for this market will be Spotify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment reflects extreme fragmentation in the race for Spotify's #2 global artist by monthly listeners as April 2026 closes, with Bruno Mars' explosive surge to 137 million listeners—fueled by his chart-topping single "I Just Might"—locking the top spot and sidelining his own slim 1.9% odds for second. The Weeknd edges as the faint frontrunner at 3.9% on enduring catalog streams from "Blinding Lights" and recent daily chart strength (four top songs on April 28), differentiating him from static challengers like Rihanna (110 million listeners), Bad Bunny, and Taylor Swift amid tight competition from unlisted acts like Justin Bieber (133 million). Final-hour streaming spikes and playlist adds remain pivotal amid volatile end-month shifts.

markets.question: Will Anthropic have the highest estimated revenue for Apr 27–May 3, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the model family that has the highest total estimated inference revenue based on Anera's data published at https://anera.markets/intelligence/model-family for the specified week.

The week’s values will be determined by summing the respective companies' estimated revenue figures for each day within the specified period. If data for all specified days is not released by 7:00 PM ET on the second day following the final date, this market will resolve based on data available at that time. Entries labeled “Other” will not be considered a model family for the purposes of this market. Revisions to published data will be considered only until all relevant data for the specified period has been released.

Model families will be ranked primarily by their total estimated inference revenue over the specified seven-day period, with alphabetical order of model family names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if two model families are exactly tied, “Anthropic” would be ranked ahead of “OpenAI”). This market will resolve based on the model family that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is data published by Anera Intelligence at https://anera.markets/intelligence/model-family. If the relevant data for the full period is not available by 7:00 PM ET on the second day following the final date, this market will resolve based on data available at that time. If this resolution source is unavailable, this market will remain open until the data becomes available and will resolve based on the first subsequent check.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Anthropic at 99.4% implied probability for highest weekly revenue April 27-May 3, driven by its recent surge past OpenAI's $25 billion annualized run rate to $30 billion in early April, fueled by explosive enterprise adoption of Claude models like Opus 4.7 for coding, agents, and vision tasks. Major cloud commitments from Amazon ($25 billion) and Google ($40 billion) secure massive compute capacity, while eight of the Fortune 10 rely on Claude for high-value workflows, delivering 80% enterprise revenue with long-term contracts and lower training costs than rivals. Resolution nears May 3; realistic challenges include a sudden OpenAI consumer API spike from GPT-5.5 or Google's Gemini integrations, though Anthropic's sticky business model and momentum make upsets improbable.

markets.question: Will the Ethiopian Citizens for Social Justice (EZEMA) win the most seats in the 2026 Ethiopian general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Ethiopia on June 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Ethiopian House of Peoples' Representatives election. 

If the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Ethiopian Parliament.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Ethiopian government, specifically the National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) (nebe.org.et/en).

eventMetadata.context_description: Prosperity Party's 94.5% implied probability as Ethiopia parliamentary election winner on June 1 reflects trader consensus on its incumbency dominance in the first-past-the-post system, bolstered by over 50 million voter registrations announced last week by the National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE). Recent opposition setbacks, including TPLF's revoked legal status since May 2025 and April 20 announcement to restore pre-war Tigray administration amid Pretoria Agreement tensions, have marginalized challengers like GPDP, EZEMA, NaMA, and TPLF. Fragmented ethnic coalitions and lingering Amhara-Oromo conflicts limit rivals' mobilization, favoring PP's nationwide structure. Scenarios challenging this include widespread violence prompting delays, major scandals, or unified opposition surge in key constituencies, though historical patterns suggest low upset risk.

events.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs are relegated from the English Premier League after the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club is officially relegated by the English Premier League following the 2025–26 season, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Burnley and Wolverhampton Wanderers are mathematically relegated from the Premier League after dismal campaigns, leaving one final drop spot intensely contested by Tottenham Hotspur (34 points, 18th), West Ham United (36 points, 17th), Nottingham Forest (39 points, 16th), and Leeds United (40 points, 15th). West Ham's dramatic stoppage-time victory over Everton four days ago created a crucial two-point cushion over Spurs, who snapped a long winless streak by beating Wolves but grapple with an injury crisis under Roberto De Zerbi. Nottingham Forest's recent 4-1 thrashing of Burnley bolsters their buffer via superior goal difference (-4 vs. West Ham's -16). Gameweek 35 looms pivotal: Spurs face Aston Villa away, West Ham visit Brentford, Forest travel to Chelsea, and Leeds host Burnley, with head-to-heads like Tottenham vs. Leeds next shaping survival amid tough run-ins.

markets.question: Will there be at least 2000 measles cases in the U.S. by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: As of April 23, 2026, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) reports 1,792 confirmed measles cases year-to-date in the United States across 37 jurisdictions, with recent weekly incidence slowing markedly—from 102 cases in the week starting March 22 to just 7 in the week of April 19—reflecting intensified public health responses in key outbreak areas like South Carolina (668 cases), Utah (425), and Texas (180). Over 93% of cases link to 22 ongoing outbreaks, predominantly among unvaccinated individuals (92% of total), underscoring transmission risks in communities below the 95% MMR vaccination threshold for herd immunity; hospitalization stands at 6% with no fatalities. Weekly CDC surveillance updates, typically released Thursdays via rash onset data, will provide critical epidemiological indicators through May 31 resolution, when year-to-date totals determine outcomes amid potential for imported cases or vaccination gaps to reignite spread.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a dominant 75% implied probability against a Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, driven by timeline compression following confidential S-1 filings in January and reportedly April, which typically precede public prospectuses by 60-90 days amid SEC review. A missed March debut target, flagged by the CTO, underscores execution risks tied to profitability challenges, with ~$800M revenue strained by gamer-focused monetization versus enterprise benchmarks like Slack's $2.5B at higher multiples. Sub-$15B resolution odds at 21% reflect compressed private secondary valuations of $7-10B amid cratering SaaS multiples, while upside buckets languish below 3% absent revenue acceleration. Key catalysts: imminent public S-1 drop and equity market reception for tech listings.

markets.question: Will Mark Dixon win the 2026 Watford mayoral election?

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next mayor of Watford as a result of this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by April 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the Watford Borough Council.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Liberal Democrat Mayor Peter Taylor commands 93.5% trader consensus in the Watford Mayoral Election set for May 7, driven by his strong re-election bid following a March 30 manifesto launch emphasizing local delivery and a decade-plus of party dominance under first-past-the-post voting. Fragmented opposition—six challengers including Labour's Keith Morgan (3.1%), Conservative Abdul Laskar (1.4%), and independents—splits anti-incumbent votes, reinforcing his lead amid recent hustings showcasing council achievements. While odds imply near-certainty reflecting skin-in-the-game wisdom of crowds, low-turnout surprises, a late scandal, or unified challenger momentum could narrow the gap before polls close.

markets.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $82,000 on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights hold a 58.5% implied probability as trader consensus in their tied 2-2 Western Conference First Round Stanley Cup Playoffs series against the Utah Mammoth, driven by home-ice advantage for pivotal Game 5 at T-Mobile Arena. Shea Theodore's overtime winner in Monday's 5-4 Game 4 road victory showcased Vegas' resilience and depth despite missing center William Karlsson (lower body), with Jack Eichel and Mark Stone fueling the top lines. Utah's Karel Vejmelka boasts a strong 2-2 record, .899 save percentage, and first home playoff win via Lawson Crouse's two goals in Game 3, but the expansion Mammoth face a battle-tested Knights squad with superior playoff experience and crowd support in a potential series-shifter.

markets.question: Spread: FC Barcelona (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Barcelona" if FC Barcelona win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Bayern München".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Barcelona vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona Femení hold a commanding position in Liga F with a near-perfect 24-0-1 record, propelled by recent thumpings of Real Madrid including a 3-0 quarter-final win on March 29 to secure their UEFA Women's Champions League semi-final spot against Bayern Frauen. Bayern, unbeaten domestically this season atop the Frauen-Bundesliga save for a 7-1 group-stage drubbing by Barcelona in October 2025, advanced via a gritty 5-3 aggregate over Manchester City with late strikes from Viggósdóttir and Dallmann. First leg on April 25 in Munich favors Bayern's home crowd and revenge motive, but Barcelona's depth shines despite Aitana Bonmatí's ongoing fibula absence until early May; Bayern miss winger Klara Bühl (muscle injury). Head-to-head favors Barça (4-1-0), underscoring trader focus on aggregate dynamics, clean sheets, and Salma Paralluelo's form.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Cyber Hero and Magic in the BetBoom Rise of Legends 1st Division, initially scheduled for April 29 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Cyber Hero and Magic play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: Cyber Hero vs Magic (BO3) - BetBoom Rise of Legends 1st Division
events.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Cyber Hero and Magic in the BetBoom Rise of Legends 1st Division, initially scheduled for April 29 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Cyber Hero" if Cyber Hero win the match against Magic.

This market will resolve to "Magic" if Magic win the match against Cyber Hero.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In BetBoom Rise of Legends Season 10 1st Division's double round-robin BO3 main stage, CyberHero enters as the consensus favorite with a solid 2-1 match record (4-3 games) and third-place standing, fueled by a gritty 2-1 win over Aura Farmers on April 28 following an early 0-2 loss to undefeated Team Spirit. Stable roster featuring recent jungler addition Bert bolsters their macro execution and adaptability in group stage play. Winless Magic languishes at 0-3 (2-7 games), hampered by narrow defeats to Virtus.pro (1-2), Verso Time (0-2), and FORZE Esports (1-2), exposing vulnerabilities in late-game execution and roster synergy that limit upset potential in this pivotal matchup.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Karachi be 32°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Masroor Airbase Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Masroor Airbase Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pk/karachi/OPKC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Kane's commanding 33-goal tally for champions Bayern Munich has locked in trader consensus at 99.5% implied probability for the Bundesliga top goalscorer crown, a 15-goal cushion over VfB Stuttgart's Deniz Undav on 18 amid just a handful of matchdays left. Recent strikes in Bayern's April 25 rout of Mainz and April 19 win over Stuttgart extended his record-breaking campaign—the most goals by any player across Europe's top leagues—while Bayern clinched the title on April 20, easing title pressure. Rivals like Union Berlin's Mohammed Amoura (recent hot streak pricing him at 0.7%) or Dortmund's Serhou Guirassy trail far behind; only a catastrophic Kane injury preventing all remaining appearances or freak multi-hat-tricks by challengers could shift the market, scenarios bettors deem negligible.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Spirit Academy enters this Exort Series #25 Main Stage Round 1 BO3 matchup as the higher-ranked squad, backed by their parent organization's resources and a 3-1 Contenders Stage qualification featuring strong wins on Nuke and Overpass. Their roster, including riflers VILBy and mazay alongside AWPer Kiryasoo, has shown consistent map control in recent European online events, building momentum toward a tournament runner-up finish. Wave Esports, qualifying 3-1 from Contenders despite a weaker overall profile (world #516), fields stand-ins cMS and pxsl amid March losses at Urban Riga Open and Esplay Backforce Masters. Traders weigh Spirit's firepower edge against Wave's upset potential in swiss format volatility, with standard CS2 map pool (Dust2, Mirage, Ancient played) favoring experienced executes.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 240-259 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This moderate baseline, drawn from ongoing short-term markets and April's frontrunner at 1040-1079, anticipates sustained engagement amid Tesla updates, SpaceX milestones, or political buzz that historically spike his viral output—past months like March hovered near 1300. Closely matched top bins underscore uncertainty: steady cultural commentary could cap at 1000-ish, while high-engagement events (e.g., product reveals) might push toward 1200, with traders weighing his unpredictable social media rhythms against quieter periods. markets.question: Will ByteDance have the top AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model with the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $6,200 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

series.title: Kharg Island oil terminal be hit

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid escalating US-Israeli strikes against Iran in the ongoing conflict, Kharg Island—handling nearly 90% of Iran's oil exports—saw US forces target military sites including air defenses, radar stations, and docks on April 7, but explicitly spared the oil terminal itself to avoid broader economic fallout or environmental risks. President Trump's prior threats to seize or obliterate the facility have not materialized into direct hits on oil infrastructure, with focus shifting to naval blockades in the Strait of Hormuz, causing Iran daily losses exceeding $500 million. Ceasefire talks falter as Washington rejects Tehran's proposals, raising risks of renewed airstrikes, though traders weigh de-escalation signals and historical reluctance to disrupt global oil flows amid stalled negotiations and Operation Economic Fury. Resolution hinges on verifiable kinetic strikes on the terminal by April 30, 11:59 PM ET.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: Team Flash vs AC Esports (BO3) - MPL Malaysia Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Team Flash and AC Esports in the MPL Malaysia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 1 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Flash" if Team Flash win the match against AC Esports.

This market will resolve to "AC Esports" if AC Esports win the match against Team Flash.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will Xiaomi have the second-best Coding AI model at the end of April 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Israel x Hamas ceasefire cancelled by June 30?
markets.description:
On October 9, 2025 Israel and Hamas signed a deal implementing a ceasefire: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israel-hamas-agree-gaza-ceasefire-return-hostages-2025-10-09/

This market will resolve to "Yes" if a either Israel or Hamas announce the cancellation of the ongoing ceasefire, or if a consensus of credible reporting confirms that the ceasefire is no longer in effect, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

Announcements that one side or another violated the ongoing ceasefire will not be enough to resolve this market to "Yes"; only a definitive announcement by Israel or Hamas, or otherwise a wide consensus of credible reporting that that the ceasefire has been ended, will qualify to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary solution resolution source will be official information from Hamas or the government of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Israel-Hamas ceasefire, part of the US-brokered Gaza peace plan initiated in October 2025, remains in effect but highly fragile six months on, with mutual accusations of violations driving trader caution. Israeli forces report five Hamas breaches in mid-April amid ongoing airstrikes and operations in Gaza that have killed dozens of Palestinians, while Hamas demands guarantees of full troop withdrawal before disarmament discussions. Recent US-Hamas direct talks and Hamas signals on relinquishing some weapons have stalled over Gaza governance and aid shortfalls. Regional ceasefires with Lebanon and Iran have eased broader tensions, refocusing pressure on these negotiations, with no major escalation yet but potential for breakdown if violations intensify.

markets.question: Map Handicap: NAVI (-1.5) vs Karmine Corp (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Natus Vincere and Karmine Corp in the VCT EMEA Group Alpha, initially scheduled for April 30 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere wins 2 or more maps than Karmine Corp in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Karmine Corp". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Map Handicap: NAVI (-1.5) vs Karmine Corp (+1.5)

events.title: Valorant: Natus Vincere vs Karmine Corp (BO3) - VCT EMEA Group Alpha
events.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Natus Vincere and Karmine Corp in the VCT EMEA Group Alpha, initially scheduled for April 30 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against Karmine Corp.

This market will resolve to "Karmine Corp" if Karmine Corp win the match against Natus Vincere.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In VCT EMEA Stage 1 Group Alpha's single round-robin, Natus Vincere enters their Week 5 BO3 clash with Karmine Corp at 1-3 (4-7 maps), clinging to faint playoff hopes as the top four advance, while winless 0-4 Karmine Corp (3-8 maps) fights elimination after recent 1-2 losses to FUT Esports and Team Heretics. NaVi's April roster refresh—adding IGL chloric and ExiT amid Shao's inactivity—yielded their lone 2-1 upset over Heretics but stumbles since, including 1-2 to Team Liquid last week; Karmine Corp swapped sheydos for N4RRATE in March yet falters defensively with poor round differentials (-27). NaVi holds head-to-head edge from a 2-0 Kickoff win, but both squads' shaky map pools and meta adaptation challenges keep this matchup competitive for trader consensus.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-12 House seat?

This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

events.title: IL-12 House Election Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Mike Bost secured the Republican nomination unopposed in the March 17, 2026, Illinois primary for the safely Republican 12th Congressional District, facing Democrat Julie Fortier, who also ran without opposition. Trader consensus at 93.5% for Republicans reflects the district's strong GOP lean in southern Illinois, Bost's history of comfortable victories—including wide margins in recent cycles—and lack of competitive polling or fundraising signals favoring Democrats amid national generic ballot trends showing only modest Democratic edges elsewhere. Absent a major scandal, health event for Bost, or unprecedented turnout surge in this rural battleground, the seat remains a low-risk Republican hold ahead of the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Atlanta be between 82-83°F on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 29 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ga/atlanta/KATL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Official Weather Underground observations from Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport (KATL) station confirm the highest temperature on April 29 reached 82-83°F, solidifying trader consensus at a 100% implied probability for that outcome amid $90K+ in volume. This peak, recorded under mostly cloudy skies with light southwest winds and 1+ inch of precipitation, exceeded National Weather Service pre-event projections of 79°F but stayed within historical April norms averaging 77°F (record high 88°F). Supporting factors include mild southerly flow and urban heat effects in Atlanta. Realistic challenges are minimal—only rare post-processing revisions from NOAA quality control could shift it, though preliminary data aligns tightly.

markets.question: Will Alex Lawson win the 2026 Ottawa mayoral election?
markets.description:
The 2026 Ottawa mayoral election is currently scheduled to be held on October 26, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next mayor of Ottawa as a result of this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from Ottawa.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Mayor Mark Sutcliffe commands 63% trader consensus for re-election in Ottawa's October 26 municipal election, buoyed by incumbency advantages and a fragmented opposition field despite a Liaison Strategies poll released April 28 revealing net negative approval ratings—45% approve overall, with majorities disapproving of his handling of housing affordability, crime, and the controversial Lansdowne 2.0 redevelopment plan. Kitchissippi Coun. Jeff Leiper trails at 18% as the most credible challenger among declared or exploring candidates, while homebuilder Alex Lawson (4.5%), former 2022 runner-up Catherine McKenney (3.6%), and economist Neil Saravanamuttoo (0.3%) split progressive and anti-establishment votes. Nominations open in May, with no head-to-head polling yet to consolidate challengers.

markets.question: Will Arouca qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Conference League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Conference League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Conference League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Conference League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 Primeira Liga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Conference League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or the Primeira Liga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
