markets.question: Will ForecastEx self-certify sports event contracts by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Which DCMs self-certify sports event contracts by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: CFTC's Division of Market Oversight issued a pivotal advisory on March 12, 2026, outlining enhanced standards for designated contract markets (DCMs) to self-certify sports-related event contracts, emphasizing pre-certification engagement with sports leagues, official data for settlement, and robust manipulation risk analysis to comply with core principles. This followed MLB's memorandum of understanding with the CFTC, signaling regulatory openness to prediction markets on outcomes like game winners or player stats, distinct from traditional sports betting. No major DCM self-certifications for such contracts have occurred in the past 30 days amid ongoing court affirmations of CFTC jurisdiction, but traders eye CME's MLB ties as a frontrunner ahead of the June 30, 2026 deadline, with potential filings from others like CBOE or ICE hinging on league partnerships and public comments on pending rulemaking.

markets.question: Will USA win Group D in the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins Group D in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage, scheduled for June 11-27, 2026.

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: FIFA World Cup Group D Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins Group D in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage, scheduled for June 11-27, 2026.

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices USA at 37% and Türkiye at 35% to win Group D, reflecting a fiercely competitive group stage battle shaped by recent head-to-head results and qualification form. The USMNT, leveraging co-host home advantage across venues like SoFi Stadium, holds a slight edge after 2-1 friendly wins over Paraguay and Australia last fall, but their 2-1 loss to Türkiye in June 2025 underscores the Europeans' counter-pressing threat led by Hakan Çalhanoğlu. Paraguay's stingy CONMEBOL qualifying defense (fewest goals conceded) bolsters their 17% implied probability for an upset push, while Australia's physicality and high press linger as a wildcard despite low 6.9% odds. Ongoing training camps in California emphasize set-piece duels and acclimation, keeping the race tight with no major injuries reported.

markets.question: 50m views on a MrBeast video in the first day by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the number of views any YouTube video posted by MrBeast gets in the first day after being posted is greater than or equal to 50 million between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If MrBeast does not post a YouTube video by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this is MrBeast's YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast), specifically the 'views' counter for the described video.

Note: This market refers to MrBeast's videos. Shorts, previews, or other videos released outside of this market's timeframe will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 93.5% implied probability, driven by MrBeast's sustained sub-50 million first-24-hour view counts across 2026 main channel uploads, including 33.9 million for "50 Streamers Fight for $1,000,000" in early April and 37.9 million for "Last to Leave Grocery Store, Wins $250,000" around April 21. Despite a staggering 480 million subscribers, his bi-weekly cadence, pivot to longer-form streaming series like Beast Games on Prime Video, and lack of blockbuster teases have capped initial YouTube velocity amid potential audience saturation. No qualifying video has emerged in over a month, with his Gaming channel topping out at 16 million day-one. Realistic upsets could stem from an unannounced mega-collab or the teased Alaska challenge premiere before May 31, though historical patterns suggest low odds of breaking the 50 million barrier.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls for Brazil's October 4, 2026, presidential election first round consistently show President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva leading with 37–47% of voting intentions, but short of a majority, positioning Senator Flávio Bolsonaro as the clear runner-up at 30–40% by consolidating right-wing support amid his father's ineligibility. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects this polling trend, pricing Flávio at 65% implied probability for second place, while Lula's 19.5% accounts for Flávio's surge narrowing the gap to as little as 7 points in the latest AtlasIntel survey (April 22–27). Fragmented fields leave governors like Ronaldo Caiado and Romeu Zema distant at 3–6%, with no major shifts in the past week amid ongoing campaign positioning ahead of party conventions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors an 18°C high temperature in Shanghai today at 56.5% implied probability, driven by the latest guidance from the China Meteorological Administration following yesterday's anomalously cold 13°C maximum at the official Xujiahui station—well below the late-April climatological average of 20–21°C. A recent cold air outbreak from northern China suppressed highs through April 29, with lingering northerly winds and partial cloud cover today limiting solar heating and capping warming potential around 18–19°C per model consensus, including ECMWF and GFS runs. Current morning observations show temperatures starting in the mid-teens, aligning with subdued daytime peaks amid low-level cool advection; resolution hinges on final Xujiahui readings by midnight, with hourly updates potentially shifting sentiment as the day progresses.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Party of Korea candidate Park Soo-hyun commands trader consensus at over 90% implied probability to win the June 3, 2026, Chungcheongnam-do gubernatorial election, driven by recent polls showing him leading incumbent People Power Party Governor Kim Tae-heum by 12-20 points, including a TJB survey from April 21 with Park at 42% to Kim's 30%. Park's momentum surged after securing the nomination in a April 15 runoff and resigning his National Assembly seat on April 29 to campaign full-time, emphasizing regional integration with Daejeon and AI-driven growth. Battlegrounds like Cheonan and Asan favor the challenger amid national Democratic Party strength. Upsets could arise from scandals, incumbent rebound via economic achievements like Silicon Valley pledges, or shifts in swing voter turnout.

markets.question: Bill Clinton divorce by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 96.4% implied probability for a Bill Clinton divorce by June 30, driven by the complete absence of any legal filings, public announcements, or credible reports of marital dissolution despite tabloid rumors in late 2025 tied to Jeffrey Epstein document releases. The Clintons' 50-year marriage has weathered past scandals like the Monica Lewinsky affair and impeachment without ending, reinforced by their recent joint appearance together with daughter Chelsea at the April 20 Boston Marathon. With two months left, high barriers including advanced age, shared legacy, and institutional privacy favor continuity, though a sudden health crisis, explosive new revelations, or irreconcilable personal rift could still trigger proceedings.

markets.question: Exact Score: Wolverhampton Wanderers FC 1 - 0 Sunderland AFC?
markets.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Wolverhampton Wanderers FC and Sunderland AFC, scheduled for May 2, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Wolverhampton Wanderers FC vs. Sunderland AFC match originally scheduled for May 2, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: SSC Napoli, defending Serie A champions and second in the standings with around 66 points after 30 matches, visit surging fourth-placed Como 1907 on May 3 at Stadio Giuseppe Sinigaglia. Como's fairy-tale season features robust home form—nine wins in 16 league games—and unbeaten recent head-to-heads against Napoli, including a 2-1 league victory earlier this term, a 0-0 draw, and a dramatic Coppa Italia quarterfinal penalty win in February. Napoli boast strong away results but grapple with injuries to Romelu Lukaku (muscle, late April), Giovanni Di Lorenzo (knee), Amir Rrahmani (thigh), and Antonio Vergara (foot), potentially disrupting their lineup amid a tight Champions League race. Both enter unbeaten in their last three Serie A outings.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Obliterated" or "Obliteration" at The Villages on May 1?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to visit The Villages on May 1, 2026, 3PM ET (https://www.dailycommercial.com/story/news/local/2026/04/28/president-trump-set-to-visit-the-villages-in-florida-on-may-1-2026/89835008007/). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during events at The Villages scheduled for May 1, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old interviews or prerecorded videos are aired where Trump is speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g., joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however, "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market is explicitly about Trump's visit to The Villages scheduled for May 1, 2026, 3PM ET. Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of the named, scheduled event will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If this event is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, "-No Qualifying Event-" will resolve to "Yes" and all other brackets will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will NYC Mayor post 200+ posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani (@NYCMayor), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Sao Paulo be 29°C on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 29 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/br/guarulhos/SBGR.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Realistic challenges include rare post hoc data revisions from quality control or alternative station measurements, though final INMET bulletins rarely alter peaks this close to reporting.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic commands a 64.5% implied probability as the frontrunner for the top large language model (LLM) on the LM Arena Chatbot Leaderboard's Text Overall rankings by May 31, propelled by Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7's recent dominance in ELO scores around 1500-1750, excelling in agentic coding benchmarks like SWE-Bench Verified (over 80%) and real-world tasks due to enhanced training data emphasis. Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro trails at 29.5%, buoyed by strong multimodal reasoning but lagging in coding efficiency. OpenAI's GPT-5.x has slipped to 5.1% amid underwhelming updates relative to competitors. Traders eye potential pre-deadline releases from Google DeepMind or xAI as catalysts, though Claude's lead reflects aggregated skin-in-the-game consensus on sustained capability edges.

markets.question: Newcastle United FC vs. Brighton & Hove Albion FC: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game between Newcastle United FC and Brighton & Hove Albion FC, scheduled for May 2 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Newcastle United FC and Brighton & Hove Albion FC combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Newcastle United FC vs. Brighton & Hove Albion FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for May 2 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Newcastle United host Brighton & Hove Albion at St. James' Park in Premier League Matchweek 35, with both sides vying in the mid-table scrum—Newcastle around 14th after 33 games amid a tough run including recent losses to Crystal Palace and Bournemouth, while Brighton sit higher near 9th following a 2-2 draw at Tottenham. Newcastle's injury woes persist, with captain Bruno Guimarães pushing for a return from hamstring trouble and illness after two months out, though Fabian Schär's foot infection sidelines him into early May; Brighton's list is lighter despite Diego Gómez's recent knock. Home advantage and potential returns could boost the Magpies, but Brighton's stronger recent form and head-to-head edge, including a 2-1 win last October, shape trader consensus on a competitive clash.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's closely contested March 22 parliamentary elections, where Robert Golob's Freedom Movement narrowly led Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) with 29 seats to 28 in the 90-seat National Assembly, Golob failed to secure a coalition for the required 46-seat absolute majority after weeks of negotiations, conceding on April 20. President Nataša Pirc Musar then directed parliament on April 25 to nominate a viable prime minister candidate. A pivotal April 29 vote passing SDS-proposed Government Act reforms with 49 votes signals Janša has assembled sufficient coalition support, driving trader consensus to 93% implied probability for his premiership. Challenges could arise from coalition defections, a failed confidence vote, or procedural hurdles prompting snap elections.

markets.question: Will Opendoor (OPEN) close at $5.00-$6.00 on the final day of trading of the week of Apr 27 – May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Opendoor (OPEN) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Opendoor (OPEN) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OPEN/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 82% implied probability for Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) closing the week of April 27 between $5.00-$6.00, aligning closely with the stock's recent trading range of $5.19-$5.68 and intraday levels around $5.50 as of April 30. This positioning stems from fresh operational catalysts, including a record weekly home acquisitions high of 658 homes, new CEO insider buying, AI-enhanced inventory pricing boosting margins, and high-profile hires like Shopify's former Chief Design Officer under CEO Kaz. The 16.5% odds for $4.00-$5.00 reflect downside protection amid persistent housing market freeze, while 10.5% for $6.00-$7.00 anticipates upside from improving cohorts. Q1 earnings on May 7 loom as the key near-term trigger, with analysts' median price target at $5.00.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: FUT Esports vs Astralis (BO3) - BLAST Rivals Group A
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Lower bracket semifinal match between FUT Esports and Astralis in the BLAST Rivals Group A, initially scheduled for April 30 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win the match against Astralis.

This market will resolve to "Astralis" if Astralis win the match against FUT Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In BLAST Rivals Group A double-elimination format, FUT Esports and Astralis clash in a high-stakes BO3 lower bracket final, with the winner advancing to playoffs and the loser eliminated after both suffered Day 1 defeats—FUT 1-2 to top-ranked Vitality and Astralis 0-2 to G2. FUT's roster is compromised by star rifler lauNX's absence due to a stomach ulcer diagnosed days ago, forcing head coach coolio into the active lineup alongside dziugss, cmtry, dem0n, and Krabeni, potentially disrupting synergy despite their 4-1 streak entering the event and #5 VRS ranking. Astralis, featuring phzy, HooXi, jabbi, Staehr, and ryu at #7 VRS, boasts superior recent stats (1.16 rating, 82 ADR) and a deep PGL Bucharest run, though head-to-head sits even at 9-7 FUT wins. FUT excels on Mirage (71% winrate), while Astralis dominates Overpass (85%), setting up a pivotal map veto battle.

eventMetadata.context_description: Connor McDavid leads Polymarket's Hart Memorial Trophy consensus at 49.4% implied probability after clinching the Art Ross Trophy with a league-high 138 points (48 goals, 90 assists) in 82 games, powering the Edmonton Oilers into playoff contention despite inconsistent team support earlier in the season. Nikita Kucherov sits at 31.4% following his runner-up 130 points that propelled the Tampa Bay Lightning to a strong Southeast Division finish, highlighted by his dominance in a mid-April Trophy Tracker poll where he garnered 10 of 16 first-place votes. Nathan MacKinnon's 16.0% reflects his 127 points and Colorado Avalanche's Central Division success, though a late-season fade allowed McDavid to surge ahead as the regular season concluded on April 16. With NHL award finalists announcements underway and Hart voting set for May 8, trader sentiment hinges on regular-season MVP value amid playoff starts.

eventMetadata.context_description: A University of New Hampshire poll released April 23, 2026, capturing trader sentiment in the Rhode Island Democratic gubernatorial primary, shows Helena Foulkes leading incumbent Governor Dan McKee 45%-11% among likely primary voters, with Gregory Stevens at 3% and 39% undecided—widening her edge from prior surveys and aligning with her 71.5% implied probability on Polymarket. McKee faces 76% disapproval amid infrastructure and housing concerns, while Foulkes leverages strong fundraising exceeding $1 million in Q1 and name recognition from her narrow 2022 primary loss. Stevens garners minor support as a longshot challenger, and House Speaker Joe Shekarchi trails after opting out in February. The September 9 primary remains open to shifts from undecideds or debates.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Porto tops the Liga Portugal Betclic table with a commanding lead, setting the stage for a potential title-clinching victory over mid-table FC Alverca in Matchday 32 at Estádio do Dragão on May 2. Traders note Porto's dominant 3-0 away win over Alverca in December, bolstered by superior home form and head-to-head record (8 wins in 11 meetings). Key absences include Porto's Martim Fernandes (leg, out until early May) and Samu Omorodion (cruciate, late April), plus Alverca's Julián Martínez (injured). Alverca's modest away record (3-4-7) underscores Porto's edge despite rotation risks.

markets.question: US Treasury transactions on blockchain by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the US Department of the Treasury sends any funds or assets via a blockchain by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes", the payment must be a publicly announced, official transaction involving the sending of assets or funds; exploratory or experimental transactions will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (LOW) $260 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Apple (AAPL) hit Week of April 27 2026?
events.description: What will Apple (AAPL) hit Week of April 27 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Apple's fiscal Q2 2026 earnings release, scheduled after market close on April 30 with a conference call at 5 p.m. ET, dominates trader sentiment as the primary catalyst for share price movements during the week of April 27. Consensus analyst estimates project $109.5 billion in revenue and $1.93 earnings per share, signaling roughly 14% year-over-year EPS growth driven by iPhone sales rebound and services expansion. Recent April 20 leadership shifts—Tim Cook transitioning to executive chairman and John Ternus named CEO effective September 1—heighten focus on strategic updates, including AI initiatives. AAPL shares traded volatilely in a $265–$273 range early week, closing at $270.17 on April 29 amid elevated volume, with post-earnings guidance poised to influence near-term valuation multiples.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the South Carolina Senate race in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 midterm South Carolina U.S. Senate election, inclusive of any run-offs.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham's entrenched position in solidly Republican South Carolina drives trader consensus toward a GOP general election win on November 3, with the state's partisan lean and historical Senate blowouts favoring the party hold. Recent consolidation in the GOP primary field—highlighted by Paul Dans dropping his challenge on April 10 and endorsing Mark Lynch—has not dented Graham's frontrunner status, bolstered by President Trump's strong re-endorsement labeling him "fantastic" while criticizing Lynch. March polls show Graham leading generic Democrats by low-single digits despite 61% unfavorables, emphasizing Democrats' steep path amid low statewide turnout and a crowded primary field ahead of June 9 nominating contests.

markets.question: Will Girona qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Champions League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 LaLiga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Champions League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or LaLiga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lagos be 30°C on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ng/lagos/DNMM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in short-range forecasts for Lagos' highest temperature on May 1, with NiMet bulletins and AccuWeather models converging around 33–35°C amid the seasonal onset of rains. Recent NiMet outlooks highlight isolated thunderstorms over southern Nigeria, including Lagos, potentially capping peaks through morning rain and cloud cover (61% precipitation chance per AccuWeather), while sunny intervals could push toward 35°C as seen in April's 35°C highs. Historical May averages hover near 32°C, but emerging El Niño signals (80% NOAA probability by May–July) may enhance heat via warmer sea surface temperatures. Watch NiMet's final afternoon update for resolution-defining clarity.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Vasylivka by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Vasylivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.357760° N, 37.038017° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vasylivka, a village in Zaporizhzhia Oblast near Orikhiv, remains under Ukrainian control along a contested frontline west of Zaporizhzhia city, with no verified Russian entry in the past 30 days. Ukrainian forces achieved recent tactical advances in the Orikhiv direction, evidenced by geolocated footage of Russian strikes on newly gained positions north of Novodanylivka as of April 27-28. Russian troops continue offensive operations and airstrikes in the adjacent Hulyaipole direction without confirmed gains, though pro-Russian sources claimed full control of nearby Orekhovo-Vasilyevka tree lines on April 25. Ongoing counterattacks, drone disruptions to logistics, and reserve deployments shape trader assessments amid slow-moving escalation signals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's commanding 70% victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26 secured the GOP endorsement and automatic ballot qualification for the September 1 primary, propelling trader consensus to an 84.5% implied probability of him winning the nomination. Brian Shortsleeve narrowly cleared the 15% delegate threshold to advance, positioning him as the primary challenger at 13%, while former Housing Secretary Mike Kennealy's 14% showing failed to qualify him via convention, dropping his odds to 1.8% despite a potential signature petition path. Minogue's fundraising dominance, with nearly $10 million cash on hand versus competitors' far lower totals, further bolsters his frontrunner status amid calls for debates that could influence undecided Republican and independent primary voters.

markets.question: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. RC Celta de Vigo: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game between Club Atlético de Madrid and RC Celta de Vigo, scheduled for May 9 at 12:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Club Atlético de Madrid and RC Celta de Vigo combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. RC Celta de Vigo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 9 at 12:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Madrid, entrenched in fourth place in the La Liga table after 33 matches, host sixth-placed RC Celta de Vigo on May 9 at Riyadh Air Metropolitano in a pivotal late-season clash for Champions League qualification. Los Colchoneros' recent form includes gritty home wins over Athletic Bilbao (3-2) and Getafe (1-0), bolstered by strong head-to-head dominance (19 wins in 30 meetings), though injuries to José Giménez (muscle), Dávid Hancko (ankle), and Pablo Barrios (thigh) temper squad depth. Celta, winless in their last five with a 1-0 loss to Barcelona, face away form woes despite a 1-1 draw at home earlier this season; key absences like Manu Fernández (muscle) add pressure amid their Europa League push. Home advantage and table stakes shape trader consensus.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Shenzhen be 20°C or below on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shenzhen/ZGSZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects forecast models from AccuWeather and The Weather Channel indicating a high temperature of 26-28°C at Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport on May 1, closely splitting probabilities between 27°C (36.5%) and 28°C (27.5%) amid inherent short-term uncertainty. Recent heavy rainfall and extensive cloud cover through late April have suppressed daily highs to the mid-20s°C, below early May climatological norms of 28-29°C, with sea breezes moderating coastal warmth. Key differentiators include potential afternoon clearing allowing urban heat island effects to peak at 28°C, versus persistent overcast or late showers capping at 26-27°C; China Meteorological Administration updates and final model runs due within 24 hours could shift the balance before resolution via Wunderground station data.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

Scenarios narrowing the margin below 20%—such as disproportionate remaining Republican absentee ballots—appear improbable barring recounts, with certification pending.

Alexander Zverev's 9.5% reflects his proven red-clay pedigree, including the 2024 Rome title and French Open final, while Novak Djokovic (8.8%) and Carlos Alcaraz (8.0%) trail amid age-related concerns, knee recovery, and forearm/wrist setbacks limiting their spring form. Karen Khachanov (4.2%) and Daniil Medvedev (4.1%) draw niche support from clay upsets like Khachanov's 2024 RG breakthrough over Sinner, but the crowd's wisdom prioritizes Sinner's momentum absent major injury disruptions.

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (LOW) $300 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Republicanos (REPUBLICANOS) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Longtime Rep. Jan Schakowsky's May 2025 retirement opened the race, but former state Sen. Daniel Biss's March 17, 2026, primary victory over 15 rivals solidified a strong nominee with robust fundraising and local endorsements. Weak Republican opposition, including low-profile nominee John Elleson, reinforces this positioning absent national midterm dynamics. Potential shifts could arise from late scandals, health issues, or a dramatic GOP wave, though historical precedents in D+25 districts suggest high barriers to flipping. The general election is November 3, 2026.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Austin be between 88-89°F on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Austin-Bergstrom International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 29 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Austin-Bergstrom International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/austin/KAUS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Atlanta be between 56-57°F on May 1?

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ga/atlanta/KATL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Brazil at 60% implied probability to win their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C opener against Morocco on June 13 at MetLife Stadium, reflecting the Seleção's five-time champion pedigree, deeper attacking talent like Vinícius Júnior and Rodrygo, and a recent 3-1 friendly victory over Croatia on March 31. However, a wave of defensive injuries has introduced uncertainty, headlined by Éder Militão's hamstring surgery on April 28 ruling him out entirely, alongside concerns for others like Vítor Roque, weakening Carlo Ancelotti's backline against Morocco's compact defense and counter-threats. The Atlas Lions, undefeated atop CAF qualifiers with 24 points from eight wins, hold 19% for an upset echoing their 2-1 friendly triumph over Brazil in 2023, while 22% implies a draw in this neutral-venue tactical clash.

markets.question: Will monthly inflation increase by ≥1.1% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

This market will resolve to the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in April 2026 according to the monthly BLS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) which BLS reports to one decimal point (e.g. 0.4%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices April 2026 CPI month-over-month at 0.5% (32%) or 0.6% (28.5%), reflecting tight competition after March's hotter-than-expected 0.9% print—driven by a 10.9% energy surge amid Iran-related geopolitical tensions that lifted headline YoY inflation to 3.3% from 2.4%. Moderation is anticipated as oil volatility eases, but differentiation hinges on shelter costs (sticky at multi-month highs) versus core goods disinflation, with Cleveland Fed nowcasting 0.45% headline versus some composites at 0.2%. Yesterday's FOMC held fed funds at 3.50%-3.75% in a hawkish split vote, underscoring inflation risks; resolution awaits May 12 BLS release.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas wins 3 or more games than Toronto KOI in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Toronto KOI". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)

events.title: Call of Duty: Toronto KOI vs OpTic Texas (BO5) - Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers
events.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Toronto KOI" if Toronto KOI win the match against OpTic Texas.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas win the match against Toronto KOI.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpTic Texas enters as trader favorite after a dominant 3-1 quarterfinal win over Toronto KOI in the Stage 3 Minor last week, extending their head-to-head edge including a prior 3-0 victory, while recent scrims split evenly at 5-5 maps underscoring competitiveness. Both remain unbeaten in Major 3 Qualifiers—Toronto KOI 2-0 with an 86% map win rate tying FaZe Vegas atop standings after sweeps over Boston Breach and Carolina Royal Ravens, OpTic 1-0 following a 3-1 over Cloud9 New York. Toronto draws scrutiny for Insight's inconsistent SMG play amid 3v4 perceptions, contrasting OpTic's efficient stars like Shotzzy and Mercules on hardpoint-heavy metas with M15 and MPC. This online BO5 on May 2 shapes seeding for the offline Major, where all 12 CDL teams advance.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 160-179 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This moderate baseline, drawn from ongoing short-term markets and April's frontrunner at 1040-1079, anticipates sustained engagement amid Tesla updates, SpaceX milestones, or political buzz that historically spike his viral output—past months like March hovered near 1300. Closely matched top bins underscore uncertainty: steady cultural commentary could cap at 1000-ish, while high-engagement events (e.g., product reveals) might push toward 1200, with traders weighing his unpredictable social media rhythms against quieter periods. Key watch: early May announcements for momentum shifts.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lagos be 34°C on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ng/lagos/DNMM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Brian King be the Democratic nominee for UT-01?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the UT-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former U.S. Rep. Ben McAdams leads trader consensus at 71.5% implied probability for the UT-01 Democratic primary on June 23, driven by his strong 36%-23% edge over state Sen. Nate Blouin in the March Data for Progress poll of likely primary voters, superior fundraising through April, and incumbency-like name recognition from his 2019-2021 service. Blouin's 25.5% reflects progressive endorsements like Bernie Sanders' but has been pressured by backlash over resurfaced old social media posts mocking Mormons in the heavily LDS state. The April 26 Utah Democratic convention upset, where Liban Mohamed won ranked-choice delegate endorsement, minimally impacts odds as primary voters differ sharply from activist delegates; lower-tier candidates like Caroline Gleich and Brian King trail due to limited polling and visibility.

markets.question: Will Nolan McLean win the 2026 NL Cy Young Award?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 National League Cy Young Award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 275M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: US crude oil inventories fell sharply by 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million barrels in the week ending April 24—the largest draw in recent months—driven by refinery inputs rising to 16.1 million barrels per day at 89.6% utilization and imports dropping to 5.8 million barrels per day, while products supplied averaged 20.6 million barrels per day over the prior four weeks, up 4.6% year-over-year. This 1% above five-year average level follows earlier April builds amid fluctuating imports and steady production near 13 million barrels per day. Traders watch weekly EIA Petroleum Status Reports (next May 6) for sustained draws into summer driving season starting late May, with potential supply disruptions or export shifts influencing whether stockpiles reach targeted lows by the June 5 report.

markets.question: LoL: BNK FEARX vs Dplus KIA (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between BNK FEARX and Dplus KIA in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 30 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "BNK FEARX" if BNK FEARX win the match against Dplus KIA.

This market will resolve to "Dplus KIA" if Dplus KIA win the match against BNK FEARX.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest forecast models, including AccuWeather projections, indicate a high of 28°C at Chongqing Jiangbei International Airport on May 1, driving the 32.5% implied probability for 25°C or higher amid a rebound from recent cooling—highs fell to around 17°C on April 28 after thunderstorms. However, closely matched odds for 23°C and 24°C at 24.5% each reflect uncertainty from expected considerable cloudiness, afternoon showers, and lingering frontal influences in Chongqing's subtropical monsoon climate, which often moderates diurnal temperature ranges through high humidity and variable insolation. Historical early-May averages hover near 27°C, but model spreads highlight sensitivity to morning clearing or persistent overcast. Traders await China Meteorological Administration hourly updates for resolution-defining observations.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices an 87% chance against the U.S. enacting a federal AI safety bill before 2027, reflecting stalled legislative progress amid the 119th Congress despite recent introductions like the American Leadership in AI Act on April 27 by Reps. Lieu and Obernolte, which consolidates over 20 governance proposals but faces committee hurdles. The White House's March 20 National Policy Framework urged light-touch federal regulations preempting patchwork state laws—over 600 AI bills introduced at the state level in 2026—yet no bills have advanced to floor votes, echoing historical challenges in passing comprehensive tech legislation. With eight months until resolution, procedural bottlenecks, filibuster risks, and competing priorities like appropriations dim prospects for enactment.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).
